## Strategy 1 — Pet Pull

### Pull

- **Pet pull** Vyemm to the hallway all the way back at the bottom of the stairs (a rather long pull).
- The raid group should wait behind the door at the top of the stairs to avoid the **frontal AOE** on pull.
- **Healers** should wait at the bottom of the stairs.
- The **MT** waits in the area at the bottom of the stairs to taunt Vyemm while **Alzid** is distracted. (The goal is to get Alzid to reset after the summoner has run up the stairs, but keep Vyemm with you.)

### Positioning

- Turn Vyemm to be **perpendicular** to the hallway.
- Once properly turned, send in the forces to rush his side.
- Everyone needs to fight from his **belly**. He will move you around during the fight, so stay with him and don't get in front or behind him.

## Strategy 2 — Monk FD Reset

### Pull

- Have the raid sit at the top of the side stairs going to the upper level.
- Have a **monk** disband and **FD pull** him to the encounter room. When he resets he will be **single pullable**. This eliminates the heals from the Dracon.
- Have everyone move down into the room and stand on one side of the entrance against the wall leading into the arena.

### Positioning

- **Tank** will pull him down the stairs and turn him until his head is facing towards the arena and his back legs and tail are in the room.
- Everyone will attack him from the **sides**.
- **Tank healers** will need to fight him under his belly with dirges, and backup healers can joust or rotate in on the tank if they are hit with AOE.

## Pull

Have a **Defiler** pull with pet to either the pool to front left or front right of him when Tarinax passes raid (who is floating in water in one big clump). Have **Monk** rescue saving the Defiler who is ultra self-warded. Have the Monk position him in water so he doesn't frontal cone the raid, then FD — **Guardian** takes over, then raid moves in and gets under him.

## Tanking & Cures

- **Group cure poison** in every group
- Have **2 Guardians** rotate Reinforcement/reinforcement/rescue/rescue/reinforcement/reinforcement
- **Heal through Tarinax** because he will be switching targets a lot

## Adds

Have **Enchanters** under water mezzing adds every **45 seconds**.

> If anyone dies, more adds spawn — so either raid doesn't die or you get a ton of adds. **Rez people quickly** or you will wipe.

- Named is grouped with **5 skeletons**. Bring some **aoe dps** and they go down fast (each one has only **30-35k hp**).
- Summons [a chaotic elemental](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_chaotic_elemental), **53 ^^^ Heroic** adds, every so often that stun you for a period of time. You want to have someone dps these down away from the **Tank**.

Talking to Relana will grant you access to [Shattered Stillness](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Firemyst_Gully:_Shattered_Stillness), an epic instance of [The Firemyst Gully](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Firemyst_Gully). No quest is received, just the access.

## Mechanics

- Auto attack does **crushing** and **disease** damage.
- Every **34 seconds** she emotes, *"Through the portal now"*, and summons a [D'Morte preserver](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_D'Morte_preserver) (heroic).
  - The preserver will heal Othysis Muravian with **Gift of Rejuvenation** and **Superior Gift of Rejuvenation** for **12.3 million HP** if it gets close enough.
- Every **30 seconds** she emotes, *"Time for some soul searching"*, and casts a Disease DD (**Prone to Corruption**) on a random raid member.

> If the target of **Prone to Corruption** does **NOT** die to it, it spawns an add (Epic x2).
- Hundar and his adds are a different encounter from the group of amygdalans; however, he will immediately engage anyone who is on the ritual master's threat list.

## Mechanics

- She has a **3k cold AE** attack, and has an amazing health regen rate.
- Try to have the **tank** turn her away from the raid — her AE can be jousted.
- Cure **Elemental** quickly.
- Talk to him to start the encounter.

## Positioning

She has a kickback so plant against the wall. The **mushroom in the center of the room** works best since it helps avoid Wuoshi.

## Mechanics

- Every **10% HP** she will pop an **x2 add** — it's best to burn it down and then continue on the mob so that you don't become overrun by adds.
- Galiel has **mana drain** and will occasionally drain mana, so having some mana regen may be necessary.
- Adds have a total of **2,500,000 HP**.
- The Essence of Fear will AE fear every 20 seconds. (Or more depending on [Fear's Grasp](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fear's_Grasp))

- Heals from 1% to 25%
- Adds can be mesmerized
- Nukes hard on 2%

## Encounter Members

This is a group encounter. The participants are:

### Jracol Binari — Healer
- ~**1.3 million HP**
- Will periodically **group heal** for ~**200K**

### Blorgok the Brutal — Fighter
- ~**1 million HP**
- Will periodically force you to target him, at which point you are unable to change targets

### Meldrath Kloktik — Mage
- ~**2.7 million HP**
- Periodic stifle, **knockback** on melee hit (is a buff, **dispel** if you can)

### 2 Froglok Mercenaries — Fighters
- ~**200K HP** each
- Will taunt and force you to switch targets, but you are able to change back to your previous target

### Ludmila Kystov — Scout
- ~**4.9 million HP**
- Will periodically change targets and go after someone else in the raid, **charms males** on occasion

## Kill Order

> If you did **not** successfully kill **Doomcoil**, Meldrath will summon him to aid in this fight. To avoid this, you must **kill Meldrath before any of the other named NPCs**.

If you did kill Doomcoil, the recommended order is **Jracol, Blorgok, Meldrath, and then Ludmila**. The froglok mercenaries can be taken out at any point, or taken out by AoEs.

## Tactics

- **DPS** should target **Jracol** directly as the **MT** (and others) will be taunted to Blorgok and unable to change back.
- When other members of the raid are targeted by **Ludmila** they should run to the MT to make it easier for the tank to recover aggro.
- Alternative: **offtank Blorgok** away from raid to avoid people getting taunted too much, then full DPS first on the **frogs** (they die fast) and then the **healer** (must die fast or his heals make the fight harder).
- The **MT** has a lot to do keeping aggro on **Ludmila** — if aggro is an issue, use a second tank to hold the current kill target, and have the raid hold back AoEs.

## Pull

- One person needs to loot and equip [a cryptic device](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_cryptic_device) dropped from the Uthtak/Aktar fight.
- They can then right-click on Atrebe's Statue and select **"Press Glowing Gem on the Cryptic Device"** to make Atrebe's Statue attackable.

## Preparation

- Maximize **Poison & Magic resists**.

## Mechanics

Joust the two AoEs, which increase in damage the closer you are to the mob. **Cure immediately** with healers or potions when hit with either one.

### Sebilisian Sporecloud

- At **0 to 10 meters**: 12338-24492 poison damage plus 2903-5763 poison damage every 5 seconds
- At **10 to 15 meters**: 7583-11374 poison damage plus 2166-3250 poison damage every 5 seconds
- At **15 to 75 meters**: 4875-5958 poison damage plus 1462-1787 poison damage every 5 seconds
- For ACT the spell needs to read **"Statue's Sebilisian Sporecloud"** and goes off at about the **45 sec.** mark

### Force of Atrebe

- You will be **launched into the air** and hurled toward Atrebe's Statue
- At **0 to 10 meters**: 7936-23808 magic damage plus 3174-9523 magic damage every 9 seconds
- At **10 to 15 meters**: 5079-11851 magic damage plus 1905-4444 magic damage every 9 seconds
- At **15 to 100 meters**: 3809-5714 magic damage plus 1693-2540 magic damage every 9 seconds
- For ACT the spell needs to read **"Statue's Force of Atrebe"** and goes off at about the **40 sec.** mark

> **Force of Atrebe** actually kicks you in the direction you are facing — if you face the mob it will pull you to him; if you face away you will get a standard knockback.

## Notes

- Resists, fast cures for the dots, jousting, and a bit of luck (if both AoEs go off at the same time or very close, it is very likely that it kills MT) are the keys to this fight.
- If healers cannot cure fast enough then bring potions.

## Overview

After the engineer goes down for the first time, **one decent healer can keep one decent MT alive** vs. the Named.

## Pull

- **MT** pulls the named anywhere convenient.
- **Raid assists the MA.** On pull, MA should target a **"generator"** that has appeared somewhere around the circle that the named is flying around.
- After the **"generator"** is down, take out the **engineer**.
- After the engineer is down, keep him down with a couple of **melee dps'ers** on him.

## Generators

**"Generators"** spawn every **~20 seconds**:

- **1** for the first **20%** of the named's health.
- **2** for the next **20%**.
- **3** spawn every ~20 seconds for the rest of the fight.

> If any "generator" is still standing after **15 seconds** of their appearance, the Named will do a **KB** that will send everyone flying all over the place. The named will also **heal himself** a good amount.

## Notes

The fight comes down to having the DPS to take out the generators when they start spawning **3 at a time**.

## DPS Requirements

- DPS on Nexona must be kept **greater than 10k** at all times in order to avoid a nasty AE.
- An additional AoE, **Noxious Rot**, can be avoided if DPS is kept **greater than 25k**.

## Pull & Positioning

- Pull Nexona down the ramp to the **left corner** (left upon entering the room). **MT** group stays down there.
- Assign **4 "shiny" collectors**:
  - One at each weapon rack on either side of the room on the main floor.
  - One outside in the hallway.
  - One up top where Nexona is — three shinies spawn up there: in the middle back, and **BEHIND** each of the two specimens.
- Rest of the raid go up the ramp that is to the left when entering the room and stack on the **"specimen"**.
- **MT** cleric hammer pull or pet pull.

## Specimens

Once Nex is pulled, the entire raid force that is stacked on the **"specimen"** starts killing the specimen. Do this as fast as you can.

- Move to the other side up top where another **"specimen"** is located and take it out fast.
- Run down the ramp on the right side and take out the **"specimen"** there.
- By this time one shiny should have been harvested.

> Harvested shinies spawn an **x2 add**, and they do **memwipes**. Whoever harvested that shiny needs to let the raid know where it (the x2 add) is coming from so they can kill it.

- Leave the **"specimen"** that is in the middle of the lower section — it's of little concern.
- Move to where Nexona is being tanked and take out a **4th "specimen"**.
- The **5th "specimen"** in the middle will be consumed by Nex when she gets to **90%** health — all it does is heal her to 100%. Not worth wasting time on.

## Main Phase

- Focus on the mobs that spawn when someone harvests a shiny, and DPS on Nex in between.

## 45% — Drake Spawn

- When Nex gets to **45%** two **drake 83^^^ heroics** will spawn from the water in the lower section.
- These are easily **mezzed** for the remainder of the fight.
- Drakes should be **mezzed the whole fight** or they will stun and knock back everyone trying to kill shiny adds.
- Having a **healer** that can joust between the shiny group and the mezzers is very helpful in case one breaks.

## Burn

- Concentrate on the shiny-spawned mobs and Nexona.

## Spawn

- Minor **KB**, minor **AoE** if within **20 meters**.
- To spawn him, **click the beehive**.
- **^^ orange heroics** will come — about 4, two sets of them.
- After they are done, the **Queen Bee** will come.
- After she is dead, **Ashenclaw** will spawn.

## Fight

Minor **KB** and minor **AoE** — drag him to one of the hallways and **tank and spank**.

## Roles

- **Healers** only heal and cure and **don't do damage of any kind**.
- **1-2 people** devoted to clicking the statues in his room. Statues must be clicked **constantly** during the fight to prevent the two larger statues in the hallway leading up to his room from spawning.

## Toxic Infusion / Fatalisis

During the fight Venril will cast **Toxic Infusion**, which is an AOE that is on a **38s timer**. You have **6 seconds** to cure this **Noxious** effect from your raid.

> **Cure it or die** from a follow-up spell called **Fatalisis**. Bring potions in case you don't have group noxious cures in every group.

## 65% — Soulcube Check

At **65%** Venril will emote how he will finish the fight. Use the [Mistmyrian Soulcube](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mistmyrian_Soulcube) from [Tairiza the Widow Mistress](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tairiza_the_Widow_Mistress) within **5 seconds** or die.

## 60% — Power Management Phase

At **60%**, with Venril's main ability disabled, he will begin to watch your **power consumption**. Your raid force must keep their power around **30% or higher** to be safe.

> A raid member who gets under **30%** for too long will spawn adds that will wipe the raid most likely.

### Power Effects

On top of just maintaining your power, Venril will also place 2 kinds of effects on 2 random members every **45 seconds**. These effects last for about **10-12 seconds** and make it more challenging to keep your power in the target zone.

- **Mana Sacrifice** — causes you to consume **200% more power** than usual.
  - Do **not** use any abilities that use power while this effect is on you.
  - If you can, turn up your particle effects because you will glow **'BLUE'** when Mana Sacrifice is on you.
  - If you are in the middle of casting anything and the effect lands on you, **cancel it immediately**.
- **Enhanced Power Regen** — the opposite. It gives the target enhanced power regen. A feed of about **1% power every 2 seconds** for **10-12 seconds**.

> If you kill Sathir without the Soulcube, your entire group/raid will be hit for 100,000+ focus damage and Sathir will reset rather than dying.
